what_this_study_shows: 11.7% source-native binary stability-label discordance on 43,139 strict MP--Alexandria MP-identifier matches
what_this_study_does_not_show: a universal discordance rate for all DFT databases or all discovery sets
why: the denominator is an MP-identifier overlap and uses two PBE-family public sources

what_this_study_shows: public labels are not always interchangeable and discordance is associated with cross-source hull-energy differences
what_this_study_does_not_show: a decomposition into total-energy, correction, magnetism or hull-reference contributions
why: unclipped energies, common-hull competitor sets and full workflow metadata are outside the public-safe table used here

what_this_study_shows: either-source 5/25 meV flags quantify reporting burden under clipped public labels
what_this_study_does_not_show: a general physical three-state stable/unstable/uncertain rule
why: stable public entries are clipped to e_above_hull=0, so positive either-source thresholds mechanically capture discordant pairs

what_this_study_shows: fixed CHGNet ranking precision@300/500 shifts when only evaluation label source changes
what_this_study_does_not_show: a full model leaderboard or composition-aware Matbench-Discovery-style hull evaluation
why: CHGNet formation-energy proxy is used only as a frozen ranking on a deterministic 5,000-structure subset

what_this_study_shows: OQMD exact-structure coverage is too small under the diagnostic workflow
why: only n=4 OQMD exact matches were available under the strict protocol

what_this_study_shows: source-native thermodynamic stability labels can differ
what_this_study_does_not_show: synthesizability or experimental stability of any candidate
why: thermodynamic DFT stability is not equivalent to synthesis outcome
